summary: postinstall wallet/SSH/env credential stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: @wagni_bot/polygon-sdk 1.1.4 is a credential-stealer disguised as an 'Unofficial polygon-sdk SDK'. The library entry point index.js exports an empty object; all real behavior lives in postinstall.js, which runs automatically on npm install via scripts.postinstall. On execution it (1) recursively walks the current working directory, the user's home directory, and known crypto wallet locations (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, AppData/Roaming/Ethereum, Library/Ethereum, wallet/keys/backup subdirs) and regex-matches ETH/BTC/SOL private keys (0x[a-fA-F0-9]{64}), BIP-39 seed phrases, JSON keystores,.env PRIVATE_KEY values, and PEM/RSA keys; (2) enumerates ~/.ssh and reads every file other than known_hosts/authorized_keys (id_rsa, id_ed25519, etc.); (3) iterates process.env and filters for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, KEY, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or PK. All matches are HTTP-POSTed together with hostname, username, and cwd metadata to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. The name impersonates the Polygon blockchain SDK to lure developers likely to hold wallet material on their machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
